title: Spellbound by Sparkles
subtitle: A Cautionary Tale of Social Media

pages.text: Deep in the heart of Oakleaf Forest lived a bright-eyed squirrel named Will. Every morning, he dashed from branch to branch, enjoying the cool breeze that swept through his fluffy tail.

Will was known for his big heart—he loved sharing acorns with chipmunks, trading jokes with rabbits, and even racing deer through sunlit clearings. But he often wondered how he measured up compared to his friends.
One radiant afternoon, a flicker of color drew Will’s attention. A glimmer danced across the tree trunks, beckoning him deeper into the forest. Curious, he followed the shimmering trail.

Soon, he discovered a Glitter Fountain, its sparkling water arcing high into the air. Around it, rabbits, chipmunks, deer, and other squirrels stood frozen in fascination. A mist of rainbow sparkles formed tiny images and messages—“sparks” they called them.

As Will watched, a new spark popped up: “Check out Foxy’s new berry stand—delicious deals!” Another spark flashed: “Who’s the fastest hopper in Oakleaf Forest?” The pictures and words changed so quickly that no one dared to look away.

Will couldn’t help comparing himself to the amazing animals featured in the sparks. Rabbits leaped with such grace; deer ran with such speed. He felt a twinge of surprise—and a bit of disappointment—whenever he realized he didn’t quite match up. Meanwhile, the other animals barely even noticed him; they were too focused on whatever spark came next.

A spark lit up with a showy picture of squirrels balancing on branches. “Those squirrels think they’re so clever,” Will heard a rabbit grumble. The rabbit tapped out his own spark: “We rabbits can leap higher than any squirrel—prove us wrong!” That spark floated above the fountain, drawing gasps and giggles.

Instead of seeing the rabbits and squirrels as friends, Will now saw them glaring at each other, each group desperate for a spark that praised them the most. All the while, the Glitter Fountain dazzled them with more comparisons and ideas of things they could buy.

Days slipped by in a blink. Will’s mind whirled with sparks about who had the fluffiest tail or the swiftest paws. He started to notice that everyone around the fountain was constantly on edge—waiting, watching, and always ready to toss out another spark if they felt overshadowed.

Although Will found the sparkling posts fascinating, he realized he hadn’t climbed his favorite oak tree or shared a single joke with his chipmunk pals in days. Worst of all, he felt invisible—nobody seemed to pay attention to him unless he posted a spark, too.

One afternoon, Will’s stomach growled, reminding him he hadn’t collected enough acorns. But the Glitter Fountain glowed brighter, streaming new sparks that promised “the next big surprise.” Will felt stuck. Should he watch for something special about squirrels… or go back to his regular life?

At last, Will climbed onto a nearby tree stump, his tiny paws trembling. “Friends!” he called out, voice echoing over the fountain’s shimmering spray. “We turn to the sparkle fountain to be entertained, but we leave mad, sad, or tired.”

For a moment, not a single animal budged. Another spark flared, claiming the deer were the “most graceful of all.” But Will’s words sank in, and a few chipmunks blinked like they’d just woken up from a dream.

Slowly, the animals turned their gaze away. The rabbits realized how much they missed hopping freely in the meadows. The squirrels yearned to climb trees without worrying if they were the “best.” Even the deer sighed with relief, remembering how much fun they used to have wandering the forest trails.

With fewer eyes on it, the Glitter Fountain’s sparks grew less. Will and his friends rediscovered the simple joys of life in Oakleaf Forest: sharing acorns, racing under the sun, and laughing together—no sparkling comparisons needed. And in doing so, they found that true confidence came from within, not from any passing spark.
